title: Patterson's Portuguese Coronation
title_candidates: From a 10 to 168: Patterson Rewrites the Book
Two Records on the Lisbon Coast
theme: Two records, one coastline: John Patterson shrugs off a Royal Óbidos 10 to post the highest Trophy total in TEG history, while David Mullin turns the Green Jacket into a four-day procession.
opening_hook: Open on Patterson on the back nine at Praia D'El Rey in Round 2, mid-49-point demolition — then flash back 24 hours to him scribbling a 10 on the 4th at Royal Óbidos and ask how on earth those two men were the same player.
foreshadow: Patterson's R1 sextuple bogey at the 4th — planted early as the only wobble before the avalanche
Mullin's grip on the Jacket from the 18th green of Round 1 — once it's his, it never leaves
Stuart Neumann sliding to the foot of the field by the 15th of Round 1 and never climbing out
Alex Baker's see-saw of personal bests and quadruple bogeys — the most entertainingly bipolar card of the week

competitions.name: The Trophy (Stableford)
competitions.how: Patterson trailed Jon Baker by one after a messy opening 38 at Royal Óbidos (including a 10 at the 4th), then detonated the tournament with 49 points at Praia D'El Rey in Round 2 — the second-best round in TEG history — opening a 10-point lead. He held on through a scratchy 34 at Royal Óbidos in Round 3 (lead trimmed to 3), then closed with 47 at Praia D'El Rey to finish on 168 pts, an all-time TEG record, six clear of Alex Baker. Decisive moment: the 1st hole of Round 2, when he took outright lead and never gave it back.

competitions.name: The Green Jacket (Gross)
competitions.how: Mullin took the outright Gross lead on the 18th of Round 1 and never surrendered it: +15, +14, +22, +16 across the four rounds, leading by 2, then 7, then 7, then 10. Even a quintuple-bogey 9 at the 10th in Round 3 and a quadruple-bogey 8 to open Round 4 couldn't dent him. Final margin of 10 over Jon Baker, finishing +67 — an all-time TEG record.

competitions.name: The Wooden Spoon
competitions.how: Neumann was last after Round 1 (23 pts, anchored by a 9 at the 4th and an 8 at the 15th) and never moved off the bottom — pos 6 in every round. He improved each round (23, 30, 37, 37) and even mustered 16 points across holes 11-15 of Round 4, but the gap was already a chasm. Final: 127 pts, the Spoon never in serious doubt after the 15th of Round 1.

headline_candidates: Seve's Last Design Bares Its Teeth
Mullin Plants a Flag; Patterson Plants a 10
Royal Óbidos Hands Out the Bruises
rounds.chosen_headline: Seve's Last Design Bares Its Teeth
rounds.angle: A scrappy opener at Royal Óbidos: lead changes flutter through the early holes before Mullin grabs the Gross lead for keeps on the 18th; Patterson stumbles into a 10 but stays in touch; Neumann slips to the foot of the field by the 15th and Alex Baker authors the round's signature horror with an 11 at the 14th.

headline_candidates: Alex Baker's Best Day at the Office (with Caveats)
Praia D'El Rey Splits in Two
rounds.angle: The tournament's hinge round: Patterson posts 49 points — the second-best round in TEG history to date — to swing from one back to ten clear. Alex Baker, miraculously, records a personal-best 44 pts and a personal-best Gross +27 on the same card; Mullin extends the Jacket lead to seven.

headline_candidates: Back to Royal Óbidos, Back to the Grind
rounds.chosen_headline: Back to Royal Óbidos, Back to the Grind
rounds.angle: Seve's course bites everyone: Patterson's 34 is his worst of the week and his lead shrinks to three, but Mullin's quintuple bogey at the 10th somehow leaves the Jacket lead unchanged at seven. Neumann still last, the gap unbridgeable.

Patterson closes with 47 to hit 168 — the highest Trophy total in TEG history — winning by six. Mullin's final-round 16 pushes the Jacket margin to ten and the Gross total to a record +67. Alex Baker matches his Round 2 personal-best (44 pts / +27 Gross); Gregg Williams ruins an otherwise career-best week with an 11 at the 7th.

players.arc: Survives a Round 1 sextuple bogey, then plays the best two-round stretch of his TEG life (49 + 47) to set the all-time Trophy record at 168 pts and a personal-best Gross +88.
Takes the Gross lead on the 18th green of Round 1 and turns the rest of the week into a victory lap, winning the Jacket by ten on a record +67.
Runner-up on the Trophy with two personal-best rounds (44 pts twice) and a personal-best Gross total of +127 — somehow achieved while also carding an 11, two 9s and a string of quadruple bogeys.
Leads after Round 1, posts a personal-best Gross +77 and finishes Jacket runner-up — a quietly excellent week obscured by the records above him.
Posts a personal-best Gross +115 despite a sextuple bogey in three of the four rounds (9, 10, 11) — a paradox the scorecard will be asked to explain for years.
Bottom of the Trophy from the 15th hole of Round 1 to the last putt of Round 4 — improving every round (23, 30, 37, 37) but never close to escape velocity.

cuts: b22 and b23 (subsumed into Spoon narrative via b25)
Most quadruple-bogey blowups — too many to itemise, gestured at collectively as 'the Lisbon Coast tax'
venue_notes: TEG's third visit to the Lisbon Coast (2016), alternating between Royal Óbidos (Seve Ballesteros' final design, parkland with water — 2nd and 3rd TEG visits) and Praia D'El Rey (Cabell Robinson, parkland front / links-dunes back — 3rd and 4th TEG visits). Frame Royal Óbidos as the brutalist examiner (where the big numbers cluster — Patterson's 10, Alex's 11, Mullin's 9) and Praia D'El Rey as the scoring course where the tournament was won (Patterson's 49 and 47, Alex's twin 44s). Note that the writer should lean on Seve's name for R1/R3 and the Atlantic dunes of the back nine for R2/R4.
